headline: Goldman Sachs And These Banks Just Aced The Fed's Stress Test: Analyst
summary: Goldman Sachs led the Dow Jones Industrial Average higher on Monday morning after it cleared the Federal Reserve's stress test with flying colors.  Analysts also placed Wells Fargo and M&T Bank among the biggest winners of the Fed's annual test, which determines how well a bank's capital buffers can survive a crisis.  The Fed's clearance for the banks to lower their capital reserves should free up space for additional lending, dividends, stock buybacks and possibly acquisitions.

headline: Big Banks Expected to Boost Buybacks, Dividends After Stress Tests
summary: America’s largest banks will likely plan to buy back more stock and raise their dividends after the Federal Reserve releases the results of its annual stress test, aspects of which are expected to change in future iterations under the Trump administration’s broad efforts to roll back bank regulation.  “We do not expect any of our covered institutions to fail the stress test exams and believe all capital plans will be approved by the Federal Reserve,” Raymond James analysts told clients.  The reference to capital plans is critical because the test results directly affect the level of capital banks must hold and how much room they have to increase payouts to shareholders.

headline: How To Build A $1,000,000 Dividend Portfolio That Could Yield 11%+ In 15 Years
Big US banks expected to ace stress tests, boost dividends
summary: NEW YORK (Reuters) -The biggest U.S. lenders are expected to clear the Federal Reserve's annual health check this year, showing they have ample capital that can be used to boost dividends, analysts said.  The results of the central bank's so-called “stress tests” on Friday will determine how much cash lenders would need to hold to withstand a severe economic downturn.  A less strenuous methodology this year means banks will probably perform better and return more money to investors via dividends and share buybacks, analysts said.

headline: How Stablecoins Can Be Destabilizing
summary: Stablecoins’ going mainstream wouldn’t take all of banks’ deposits away.  The Senate looks set to soon pass the so-called Genius Act, which will set guidelines for issuers of stablecoins—digital tokens that are fully backed by fiat currencies such as dollars.  One big debate over the wisdom of giving stablecoins a regulatory framework centers around how they would affect the current banking system if they were to hugely expand in size.
